page: Amazon Web Services c8id.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs, corresponding to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio.

This instance utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. The combination of high-speed DDR5 memory and direct-attached NVMe storage supports high-throughput data transfers, as evidenced by a cached small-block read memory bandwidth of 5134.19 GB/sec and a large-block read bandwidth of 196.82 GB/sec.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance across multiple domains. The system achieved a Passmark CPU score of 82984.85 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 21428.0, placing it in the top 10% of tested servers. It demonstrated strong capabilities in database operations, executing 112.86 million Redis SET operations per second, and static web serving at 109.49 GB/sec. The hardware-assisted matrix extensions also enable efficient CPU-based LLM inference, processing 7B model prompts at 296.09 tokens/sec. The instance is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads, software compilation, high-performance database hosting, and localized data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory, a 480 MB L3 cache, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 30 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. The instance demonstrates top-tier performance in multi-core processing, software compilation, and database operations, achieving 112.86 million Redis SET operations per second and a Passmark CPU score of 82984.85. Advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512 accelerate matrix and vector calculations, making it highly efficient for CPU-based machine learning inference and mathematical modeling. This hardware profile offers a high-density compute resource suitable for demanding enterprise databases, web serving, and intensive CI/CD pipelines.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core CPU performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.16xlarge features 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe storage for compute-intensive workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized platform featuring high-speed DDR5 memory, local NVMe storage, and advanced matrix extensions for intensive workloads.

128.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory with 2.0 GB per core.
3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix operations.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, database, and software compilation benchmark performance.
Best suited for high-performance databases, web serving, and CI/CD pipelines.
